 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tof","title":"Create README.md for the project","description":"# Task: Create README.md

## Description
Write a clear, compelling README for the GitHub repository.

## Structure

### Hero Section
- Project name and tagline
- One-sentence description
- Key value proposition

### Quick Start
For users:
```bash
# Visit the wizard
https://acfs.dev

# Or paste this on your fresh Ubuntu VPS:
curl -fsSL "https://..." | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe
```

### What Gets Installed
- Shell environment (zsh, oh-my-zsh, powerlevel10k)
- Languages (bun, uv, rust, go)
- Coding agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
- The Dicklesworthstone stack (8 tools)

### Screenshots/GIFs
- Wizard interface
- Terminal after install
- ntm in action

### Development
For contributors:
```bash
git clone ...
cd apps/web
bun install
bun run dev
```

### Architecture
Brief overview of:
- apps/web (website)
- install.sh (installer)
- acfs/ (configs)
- packages/ (shared code)

## Do NOT Include
- Contributing section (per AGENTS.md)
- Badges (keep it clean)
- Unnecessary sections

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear value proposition
- [ ] Quick start for end users
- [ ] Development instructions
- [ ] Architecture overview
- [ ] No contributing section","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:03.971189-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:01.650172-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:01.650172-05:00","close_reason":"README.md created with comprehensive 700+ lines","labels":["documentation","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7","title":"EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery","description":"# Epic: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery

## Overview
Handle checksum mismatches gracefully by offering skip/continue options for non-critical tools instead of failing the entire installation.

## Problem Statement
If ANY of 14 upstream tools fails checksum verification, entire install fails. One stale checksum on tool #8 wastes all progress from tools 1-7.

## Business Impact
- Checksum mismatches are usually legitimate updates, not attacks
- Not all tools equally critical: missing ntm is annoying; missing bun is blocking
- Completed phases should not be wasted by tool #9 failing

## Technical Approach
1. Classify tools as CRITICAL (bun, rust, uv, claude) vs RECOMMENDED (ntm, bv, cass, etc.)
2. Critical mismatches: still abort (security-sensitive)
3. Recommended mismatches: offer interactive skip/abort/proceed options
4. Track skipped tools in state.json
5. Report skipped tools at install completion with manual install commands
6. Add --strict flag for current behavior (abort on ANY mismatch)

## Success Criteria
- Critical tool mismatch aborts with clear explanation
- Non-critical mismatch offers user choice
- Skipped tools tracked and reported
- Manual install commands provided for skipped tools

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/security.sh: Criticality classification, recovery logic
- install.sh: Use new recovery flow, track skipped tools

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 4","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:40:56.024822-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:40.596546-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:40.596546-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete - all children implemented: tool classification (v8a), checksum recovery flow (anq), --strict flag (8mv)","comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive).

Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope:
- orchestration/state/error-reporting/preflight remains install.sh + scripts/lib owned
- generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-u5z","title":"Implement system package updates (apt)","description":"## What
Implement the 'system' category for acfs-update:
- sudo apt update
- sudo apt upgrade -y (or prompt if not --yes)
- sudo apt autoremove -y

## Technical Details
- Requires sudo (script should check and prompt)
- Capture list of upgraded packages for log
- Detect if reboot required after kernel updates (check /var/run/reboot-required)
- Handle apt lock (another process using apt)

## Considerations
- This is the most impactful category (security updates)
- Should probably run first before other updates
- May need to restart services (postgres, redis) after - handle gracefully

## Success Criteria
- [ ] apt update/upgrade runs correctly
- [ ] Handles sudo password prompt (or errors gracefully if no sudo)
- [ ] Logs list of packages updated
- [ ] Warns if reboot required","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:25:38.290865-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:10:53.652006-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:10:53.652006-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented apt lock detection, upgradable package logging, reboot-required check","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-u5z","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:24.691755-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","title":"EPIC: Fix Codex CLI Authentication Documentation (OAuth, Not API Key)","description":"## Overview

This epic addresses a significant documentation error across the ACFS codebase: the incorrect assumption that OpenAI's codex-cli uses an OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable for authentication.

## The Reality

OpenAI's codex-cli uses a **built-in OAuth authentication system**, similar to how Claude Code and Gemini CLI work:

1. **Authentication Method**: OAuth via web browser or device-level code flow
2. **Accounts**: Uses ChatGPT Pro/Plus accounts (consumer accounts), NOT API accounts
3. **No API Key Needed**: OPENAI_API_KEY is for the OpenAI API, NOT for codex-cli
4. **Security Setting Required**: Must enable device code flow in ChatGPT settings (Settings → Security → API/Device access)

## Why This Matters

1. **User Confusion**: Current docs tell users to set OPENAI_API_KEY, which is:
   - Wrong (codex-cli ignores it)
   - Misleading (users may think they need an OpenAI API account)
   - Potentially dangerous (exposing API keys unnecessarily)

2. **Doctor Checks Broken**: Our health checks look for OPENAI_API_KEY, which means:
   - Legitimate users (authenticated via OAuth) will get false warnings
   - Users setting OPENAI_API_KEY will get false positives

3. **Account Confusion**: OpenAI has TWO account types:
   - **API Account**: Uses OPENAI_API_KEY, billed separately, for programmatic access
   - **ChatGPT Account** (Pro/Plus): Uses OAuth, consumer subscription, what codex-cli uses

## Success Criteria

1. No references to OPENAI_API_KEY for codex-cli authentication
2. Doctor checks correctly verify codex OAuth token
3. All documentation accurately describes the OAuth flow
4. Clear distinction between API accounts and ChatGPT accounts
5. Install messages guide users to run codex auth","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:24:54.207594-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:04.973842-05:00"}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc","title":"Implement init_state() and save_state() functions","description":"# Task: Implement init_state() and save_state() functions

## Context
Part of EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5uu)

## What to Do
Create bash functions for state management:

### init_state()
- Check if ~/.acfs/state.json exists
- If exists: load COMPLETED_PHASES array, CURRENT_PHASE, detect resume scenario
- If not exists: initialize empty state, create ~/.acfs directory
- Handle corrupted state files gracefully (warn and start fresh)

### save_state()
- Write current state to ~/.acfs/state.json atomically
- Use write-to-temp-then-move pattern to prevent corruption
- Include all tracked fields from v2 schema
- Log state save for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- init_state() correctly loads or initializes state
- save_state() writes atomic JSON (no partial writes)
- Corrupted state file detected and handled
- Functions work when jq is not installed (use sed fallback)

## Implementation Notes
- Use mktemp for atomic writes
- Consider that jq may not be installed yet (use basic parsing)
- Test both fresh install and resume scenarios

## Files to Modify
- install.sh or scripts/lib/state.sh (new)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:42:40.635486-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:38:29.61414-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:38:29.61414-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in scripts/lib/state.sh with v2 schema, atomic writes, and full lifecycle functions","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:27.650646-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-aa1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.454984-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a","title":"Classify tools as CRITICAL vs RECOMMENDED","description":"# Task: Classify tools as CRITICAL vs RECOMMENDED

## Purpose
Distinguish between tools that MUST succeed vs tools that can be skipped without breaking the system.

## Tool Classification

### CRITICAL (installation fails if these fail)
These are foundational tools that other tools or the dev workflow depend on:

```bash
CRITICAL_TOOLS=(
    "mise"      # Runtime manager - node/python/go depend on this
    "uv"        # Python package manager - many tools need it
    "bun"       # Node runtime - used by mise and web tooling
    "go"        # Go compiler - needed for several tools (lazygit, etc)
    "git"       # Already installed, but verify
    "curl"      # Needed for everything
)
```

### RECOMMENDED (skip with warning if fail)
These enhance the experience but system works without them:

```bash
RECOMMENDED_TOOLS=(
    # Shell enhancements
    "ohmyzsh"
    "zoxide"
    "atuin"
    "fzf"
    "eza"
    "bat"
    "ripgrep"
    "fd"
    "delta"
    "lazygit"
    "lazydocker"
    
    # Development tools
    "claude_code"  # Can install manually later
    "direnv"
    "starship"
    
    # Dicklesworthstone stack
    "ntm"
    "mcp_agent_mail"
    "ubs"
    "bv"
    "cass"
    "cm"
    "caam"
    "slb"
)
```

## Why Go is CRITICAL

**Rationale:**
1. `lazygit` requires Go to build
2. Several modern CLI tools are written in Go
3. If Go fails, multiple downstream tools will silently fail
4. Go installation is reliable (official binary)

## Implementation

```bash
is_critical_tool() {
    local tool="$1"
    for critical in "${CRITICAL_TOOLS[@]}"; do
        [[ "$tool" == "$critical" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}

handle_tool_failure() {
    local tool="$1"
    local error="$2"
    
    if is_critical_tool "$tool"; then
        log_error "CRITICAL tool failed: $tool"
        log_error "$error"
        log_error "Installation cannot continue."
        exit 1
    else
        log_warn "Optional tool failed: $tool"
        log_warn "$error"
        SKIPPED_TOOLS+=("$tool")
    fi
}
```

## Non-Interactive Behavior

When running non-interactively (CI, agents):
- CRITICAL tool checksum mismatch → ABORT
- RECOMMENDED tool checksum mismatch → AUTO-SKIP with warning

## Dependencies
- Used by: checksum mismatch handler (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr)
- Used by: error reporting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zm)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CRITICAL_TOOLS array defined with: mise, uv, bun, go, git, curl
- [ ] RECOMMENDED_TOOLS array for everything else
- [ ] is_critical_tool() helper function
- [ ] handle_tool_failure() respects classification
- [ ] Non-interactive mode auto-skips RECOMMENDED, aborts on CRITICAL","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:36.267827-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:43:26.105479-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:43:26.105479-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/tools.sh with CRITICAL_TOOLS (git, curl, bun, uv, go, zsh, mise, rustup, cargo) and RECOMMENDED_TOOLS arrays. Implemented is_critical_tool(), is_recommended_tool(), get_tool_classification(), handle_tool_failure(), and non-interactive auto-skip logic. All acceptance criteria met."}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-va8","title":"[codex.6] Regenerate installer scripts after fixes","description":"## Goal
After fixing the source files (doctor.sh, agents.sh), regenerate all derived scripts.

## Why Needed
The manifest generator creates scripts from the source libraries. Any fixes to:
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh
- scripts/lib/agents.sh

Must be followed by regenerating:
- scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
- scripts/generated/install_agents.sh

## Commands
cd packages/manifest && bun run generate

## Verification
After regeneration:
1. Grep generated files for OPENAI_API_KEY - should find none related to codex auth
2. Review the generated scripts for correct codex auth guidance

## Depends On
- codex.1 (doctor.sh fixes)
- codex.2 (agents.sh fixes)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All generated scripts reflect source file changes
2. No stale OPENAI_API_KEY references in generated codex checks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:02.623412-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:41:00.602163-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:41:00.602163-05:00","close_reason":"Regenerated all installer scripts.
